description_long: This is a large, roughly cubic stone block with a weathered, pale beige surface and visible chisel marks. The front face features a deeply inscribed Latin inscription, suggesting historical or commemorative significance. The top surface is uneven and slightly indented, with some erosion and discoloration visible. The sides and back are mostly plain, with one side showing a streak of mineral staining and another displaying a patch of rough, eroded texture. The block appears solid and heavy, with a coarse, granular stone texture throughout. Its overall appearance suggests it was once part of a monumental or architectural structure, possibly used as a base or pedestal.
description: A large, weathered stone block with a Latin inscription on the front and rough, eroded surfaces on the sides and top.
description_view_0: The front view shows the inscribed face with visible Latin text and vertical chisel lines.
description_view_1: This side view reveals a rough, eroded patch with discoloration and no visible markings.
description_view_2: This view displays a mostly plain surface with faint vertical streaks and minimal weathering.
description_view_3: The back view is plain and blocky, with a slightly uneven surface and no inscriptions.
attribute_location_context: ancient monument base or commemorative plinth in a historical urban square
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on a prepared stone or earth foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Noticeable surface erosion, discoloration, and rough patches, especially on the sides and top; some mineral staining is present.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible Latin inscription with deeply carved letters on the front face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears isolated but likely once formed part of a larger architectural or commemorative structure.
